concept: Intuit / Know Directly / Perceive Without Reasoning
description: To know or understand without conscious reasoning; direct perception of truth. 'Hiru' names knowing that arrives whole—not built from pieces but received complete.
sound_symbolism: The word knows directly. 'Hi' breathes with inner perception, knowing arising. 'Ru' runs with immediate flow, understanding arriving without steps.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi hiru' (I intuit). Accesses 'sorai' (insight) directly. Related to 'morae' (sense) but bypasses sensation.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Direct knowing—prajna that arises from practice, not intellectual analysis.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Intuitive knowing—the wisdom that comes from deep experience, beyond words.
